reviews.review_text:
what can you even say. anything you could previously see as perhaps diluting the pure Personality oozing out of every corner of remedy is circumvented for a game that genuinely owes twin peaks: the return as much as its more internet-recognized influences like SCP and house of leaves. their most eclectic game , yet also their most coherent. and their most Involving too, despite all the stiff competition...every ability is contextualized within the fiction with minimal membrane of gameplay abstraction, every quest gives you no more specific a marker than the room or department where you'll be able to find it, almost every physical object in the enviroment is a potential gameplay opportunity. the biggest compromise might be all the time spent on the collectables screen for lore and the Very loosely conceived loot system...never fun for the latter, but the former is still an improvement from quantum break. and ofc, almost always worth the time due to the unbelievable density and quality of these little one-page microstories...it is a gameplay disconnect, but it still pulls you further and further into the house.

ultimately the biggest source of the coherency is that just about every arc, narrative and gameplay, is in service of an emotionally paradoxical Acclimation. there is an equally admirable and unnerving quality to when jesse says she's happy to be here. both she and dylan lost their formative years to the FBC in polar opposite ways, each craving the circumstances of the other. in jesse we experience an outsider who is not just morbidly curious, but who has been waiting her whole life for this validation, who is darkly basking in the fact that she has finally stepped into a world in which she is not crazy. and in dylan we see the purest vision of the FBC without any of the romance or grounded human faces, heaped upon with traumatic special attention that leaves him particularly open to the seemingly dissociative corruption of the hiss. jesse finds relief in carving out a new identity, seemingly the only relief for dylan is to lose his identity...both equally understandable reactions to being inside the oldest house.

control in general is lacking in strong emotional signposting, and i think this is part of why some people dont know how to feel about it. but all the wiggle room it offers is so vivid that i cant imagine not taking advantage of it. most of the the various supernatural elements are presented not quite with either horror or reverence, they simply Are....documented with an attempted scientific understanding, and interacting with human perception and goals in a wide variety of ways. the observational tone of the narrative is cold, but human beings still must exist as they are within it. the characters, even the dramatically relevant ones, are not Literary at all, instead they are Presences...shades of levity and warmth as human life is essentially inherently absurd in a place not made for humans. its a little funny, and a little inspiring, and a little terrifying...control can be a profoundly lonely game, equally can it be good at reminding you that others are in the same boat you are. in that way i kinda cant believe this isnt talked about in the same way death stranding is re:covid-predictive 2019 games.

the two dlcs have a wonderful symmetry going on where they take points of tension that serve the ambiguous discomfort of the main story and inflame them to near-breaking points...but not actually Over the breaking points, crucially. part of that is they are certain to be more followed up in future games, but the dramatic wheels are greased well...foundation puts the board in a position where they can be seen not as infallible god-view narrators, but as active agents with their own goals and imperfections, capable of being knocked off-balance, and AWE puts pressure on the FBC, cataloging all its failed attempts to hold itself to any decent standard of ethics and reasonable reaction to the fact that they cannot, in fact, Control Everything. combined with all the aw2 setup, it emphasizes the complimentary spaces each story occupies....alan wake is individual, subjective, human-level, where control is about attempting to ascend beyond the human level...a task which is perhaps, ultimately incompatible with the accumulation of "worldly" power...the godlike jantior ahti on one end, the self-destructive director trench on the other. jesse, trapped in the middle, is in a fascinating place.

and all that said, i played almost 30 hours of this and tbh it still doesnt feel like enough, i might go back and do some achievement stuff eventually just to be in the house more. i love it not just as a narrative/contextual place, or a piece of atmosphere, but as a Space, the way all the departments are strung together, navigated primarily with signs and actual visual clues. and the combat is just truly killer, perhaps im just not good enough to have launch trivialize everything (as is a reported issue by many) because i felt like my whole toolkit was always in some kind of use, i even found uses for the shield by the end...and even besides, its so mesmerizing to bring this chaos to this unnervingly ordered place, and to feel like im always in some way spatially and physically interacting With The Room Im In instead of just being inside of it. i just dont relate at all to most of the popular complaints, except the loot system and radiant quests, but i tend to not like those things even when theyre "good" so i'm willing to call it a wash. and besides which its not like it actually makes the game Worse...perhaps it makes it Not As Good As It Could Be, but that seems like a silly thing to complain about when whats good is not only so potent, but so unlike anything else ive ever experienced, on any scale or budget. the black and white and red is burned into my mind, pulling a strong reaction out of me even though the nature of that reaction is completely emotionally unplaceable. its maddening. i love it a lot.

Fantastic game, and I'm only mad at myself for not playing it sooner. A worthwhile single player experience that is sorely lacking in games now. Gameplay and physics were very fun; environment is a beautiful Madhatter experience of a psychedelic concrete jungle office; world lore offered in collectible documents, audio clips, and videos are [i]actually[/i] intriguing to follow; and the writing/storytelling method is top notch. Control shook me out of a fog and made me realize just how overlooked or poor game writing can be these days.

It won't take you a hundred hours, and it doesn't need to. Just play it.

LOVE 💕 and can't wait for Control Resonance!  This game is an art piece!

One of the best put-together games I've ever played!  Play it if you like telekinesis, flying, other cool powers, and creative gun morphing!  This game is a trip, the bosses just get weirder and weirder, and the levels of the building are purposely made like a maze to fit the story, but not impossible to navigate, thanks to lots of checkpoints, which is where you level up your powers and gun morphs.

Amazing game for action-game lovers and/or for anyone who loves a good mystery and putting together a multiple stories through readable clues and recordings.  There's a ton of pick-up items and secrets strewn about the world.  

Also, story-wise, the characters and events are a great part of the Remedyverse story as a whole (including the Alan Wake series) and thanks to Sam Lake's clever writing, even Quantum Break (and Max Payne) fits as an unofficial part of this Remedyverse.  You'll get enough answers to satisfy on your basic playthrough but so many more intriguing questions if you dive deep enough.

If you haven't played this or Alan Wake🔦 start here, start there, wherever!  Join the story, if you're looking for otherworldly action with real substance🔻

[h1]Should you play Control?[/h1]
Despite me leaving a negative review for the game, I do think it is overall a serviceable game. I'd even go as far as to say that I can see how this could be someones favorite game, but it wasn't that for me, far from it. I really tried to give the game a chance and almost had the energy to get all of the achievements. In this review I'm going to go over some of my gripes with the game that [i]if[/i] you find yourself relating to can help you make the decision: [b]Is this game for me?[/b]

[h2]Story and Atmosphere (spoiler free)[/h2]
I didn't find the story that engaging. Control starts with a lot of interesting stuff thrown at you at first and you get kinda giddy about it. It throws mysteries at you that you can't wait to find answers to, but it doesn't take long to become overwhelming. Rather than slowly presenting new mysteries to the player and unveiling the answers to previous ones, the game decides to throw every mystery it holds straight at the player to the point that it is overwhelming. This happens so fast and so egregiously that you start to question whether or not you are supposed to understand what is told to you already, but  maybe you just missed the explanation. This story dump isn't helped by the fact that the developers love throwing lore files all over the map. There are some rooms where you can't move two meters without the game throwing 5 lore files at you. Now after I finished the game, I went looking around online at what others thought and they seemed to enjoy the lore the hundreds of files given to the player. Which felt weird because most of the ones I read were about something completely benign and not related to the overall story, more about the work life of the people at the bureau.

The world, however, is beautiful and the vibes are on point. The game is full of small, neat details, destructible environments that enhance the combat experience and genuinely amazing set pieces from time to time. The world feels familiar in way while at the same time creating a world that I can't say I have experienced in any other game. I think the overall experience and environments would've been enhanced even more if I wasn't mostly mentally checked out by the amount of stuff thrown at me that I won't understand until much later on in the game. When you reach the end of the game most of the questions you had at the start are answered and the story doesn't feel that complicated overall. I thought maybe I missed something thematically or story wise because I didn't read the hundreds of lore dumps. Imagine my shock when I see the people gushing about the game have the same understanding of the game as me as player who had decided not to pay much attention to it.

All of this is to say that game makes you think that it is smarter or more complicated than what it looks like. I was thrust into this constant loop of thinking "after this mission the story is gonna really pick up", but it never does. The start of the game starts way too heavy handed, and it ends up not amounting to much at the end. I can see why someone would enjoy and get more engaged to the world by this way of storytelling, but I also believe this is very alienating to many. Looking at the achievements of the game and seeing that out of the 80 percent of players who completed the first mission, only 30 percent end up playing until the final mission, I feel like this shows that I'm not the only one that felt this way. However, I feel like this is by design; it isn't meant for everyone. But for those that this kind of storytelling works, it really works.

[h2]The Gameplay and Mechanics[/h2]
The combat in this game is its standout feature. From the characters movement to the feeling of shooting at your enemies feels great. I would almost go on a limb that it just might be one of the best if not the best feeling third person shooter I have played to date. Your gun has multiple different firing modes that I found myself using all of them. The telekinesis ability to launch objects or enemies towards other enemies and seeing the aforementioned environmental destruction is never not satisfying. Cloaking yourself in debris and launching yourself towards a group of enemies is just insanely fun.

While the gameplay is fun, I think it gets bogged down by unnecessary and quite bizarre decisions made by the developers. Mainly this game has a problem with needless and poorly implemented systems regarding your skills and guns. The skill tree is bad. Mostly you can use your points to make your abilities stronger. The upgrades are as simple as "give 10% more damage". There isn't much fun to be had here. At the start of the game, I decided that I would put all my points to the launch ability, because at least the mechanic was fun to use in if itself despite the lacking skill tree. Doing this I accidentally optimized myself to way too powerful way too fast and most of the game ended up being a cakewalk. So, the skill tree ended up being mostly filled with boring upgrades and allowed me to make the game way too easy way too fast, which resulted in taking away much of the tensity and excitement from the combat encounters, trivializing them to a point. The other needless mechanic is the gun and personal modifications. I don't have anything against the idea in of itself, but managing these mods is frustrating. Enemies drop them at random and they have different buffs that range from useless to game changing. I hate the fact my character auto-picks every single one of them and I have to constantly go to my inventory to clear up all the garbage mods I get one by one.

Let’s talk about the side content. The game has radiant quests where you must go to an area of the map to destroy enemies. The reward? Mods and upgrade material (which open more mod slots to gun). These same mods I already have WAY too much to begin with are my reward. And because I'm so overpowered to begin with, clearing out these quests take less time than it takes me to fast travel to them. There are also these challenge missions? They essentially boil down to "kill this enemy type in this area with this weapon" which are horrible mainly because the chance of finding enemies in an already completed area is miniscule and the chance of the correct enemies’ spawn is even more rare. And the reward for these? A. Single. Random. Mod.

Okay, okay, those missions are not really mandatory, they are there to do when you run out of all the other stuff, I guess. What about the side missions? I think similarly to the mods some of the side missions are in a sense useless. Go here, press a button, come back. But, sprinkled in there are some that are just great. They are visually interesting and they shake things up. Then they just suddenly end, almost out of nowhere. 

[h2]Verdict[/h2]
I didn't mention this in the story section, but I hated the main story ending and the Foundation dlc ending. They felt like something was finally going to happen, we are building up something great, and then it just ends abruptly. This game for me is like the videogame equivalent to edging. The game jerks me with mystery, but it gives the relief way too late when I have already gone soft. I get to an exciting mission and can't wait for the sweet release, but it cuts off right before I'm ready to go. The game is always building up to a great climax, and for some that might work, but to me it always ends up being just shy of getting to that critical point, which is why I didn't enjoy the game. For I am not an edger — I am a gooner. 

[h3]TL;DR[/h3]
6/10

games good and the dlcs offer some nice extra content

performance notes:
- opening/closing the map or menu drops fps for a second, but otherwise the game runs well
- had to enable dlss because my mouse movement was skipping/stuttering on native res
The game has its flaws but it's still a masterpiece nonetheless. Highly recommend it and can't wait for Resonant to drop.
so fun and addictive. and beautiful. cant wait for the sequal

This game might be the most wackiest and craziest game I have ever played and I totally love it for it, after completing it 100% achievements including both the DLCs and side missions, this game is jam packed with content and is worth every single cent or rupee whatever your currency is, just go buy it and enjoy it !

Story is good, Gameplay is good, I have played this game 5 times now, rn playing it for the 6th time, each time i found a new place, hidden area.... now it runs well on my pc, you can see specs somewhere here, been playijng on custom settings, stable game now

Took a bit for me to get into the game but it's really good! The combat and skills evolve nicely and the gameplay is fun. Sometimes it can get a little bit too mazy but its not bad. The side quests can also get a bit repetitive but of course you don't have to do them. Definitely recommended.

The story and atmosphere feel like The X-Files meets Friday the 13th: The Series. On the action side, unlocking new abilities is consistently satisfying, and by the end you evolve from a federal agent into a full-on psychic powerhouse.  The biggest surprise is the setting. The Oldest House could have easily felt mundane or nonsensical, but instead it remains immersive and believable, even at its most surreal. 4/5 hiss

I'm on my second playthrough. After taking a break for a couple of years, coming back to this and reading almost all the documents I find myself enjoying this game a lot more than before., Might also be because I'm fast traveling, and on my first playthrough I barely did that. It's a great game, and I will totally get Control 2 when it gets a good discount.

This game's combat is Insanely smooth and I love it
I didnt understand the lore AND I don;t bother to read ingame files Still I did not get bored yet. The puzzles and combat are awesome. Hella fun

overall great game
the story is cool, nothing too mindblowing but its decent, and the gameplay is satisfying and feels rewarding
its sad that there are currently chuds reviewbombing the game because they wont be able to play the studio's upcoming game in their country, just because you feel entitled to everything it doesnt make it the developers' fault that youve been told no, just get a better country

Honestly one of the coolest games I’ve played in a while. The combat feels really satisfying once you unlock more abilities, especially throwing objects around with telekinesis. The whole weird paranormal vibe of the game is done really well too. Story can get confusing at times, but in a way that actually made me want to keep playing and figure things out. The world feels unique compared to most games now. Definitely recommend it if you like action games with a strange sci-fi atmosphere.
Would be better as a tv show, combat is boring and repetitive, games balancing is bad and quests are just frustrating only thing I like is the story/concept and visuals you can keep the rest
Remedy's best story driven game . Completed on ps4 back in the day , replaying with RTX

A masterpiece with a few of the usual Remedy pratfalls.

It feels like every Remedy game has some executive-driven mistake where they try to capture one of the industry’s recent gaming trends in their artsy-plot-driven-games. This… doesn’t work. Control has a whole bunch of randomly generated side content and RPG progression, a PVE multiplayer mode… all things designed to make Control a game PLATFORM that will keep you coming back. None of it works and I can’t imagine WHY it was put in the game besides that some air-brained business major who has never played a video game in their life yet owns a controlling stake in a video game company said “hey put that in it makes money because of… conversion factor and retention or something.”

Ignore all of this stuff. It doesn’t matter. Focus on the story, focus on the main game. Play the ten-hours-or-so main scenario. It’s fantastic. Remedy knocked this one out of the park.
Really like the story so far, the visuals and gameplay def recommend

runs a bit iffy but game play 7/10 visuals 9/10 (art style not the 'realism' part love the vibe it's not realistic though if that's what you are looking for)  story 9/10 (really love the freakish story line haven't finished yet but would love to see where it goes) a definate must buy for this crazy cheep price will buy the new control game if I have money which I don't. overall a 8/10 game for me so far after I finish I will give a final review also the crafting is cool and the events this is the game I needed to take a break from the toxic multiplayer games feel free to take a look at my profile.

This game is fun. Sort of like Prey but in the SCP foundation. I rather enjoy the writing and dialogue.

But it is poorly optimized for PC graphics. I have a pretty advanced graphics card and plenty of RAM, but it still was very jittery to start and required lots of futzing before I could get it to run soothly. Even still, the textures load erratically.

I love this game, it has good action based gameplay with smooth controls and simple to remember the controls aswell. If you guys love mysterious games, this game is 100% reccomended its a very good strange/interessting game, it really gets you hooked onto the game and finding all its secrets

I highly recommend this game, especially if you are familiar with SCP's, cosmic horror or just enjoy Remedy's other game series 'Alan Wake'. I also enjoy all the powers Jesse can acquire, all the extra info you can gleam from all the notes, research, Darling's little videos and all the like that you can stumble across during your experience in the Oldest House.

I'm recommending this game almost entirely on the strength of the setting. The Old House and the mountains of reports and dialog that harken to the MANY  bizarre and dangerous anomalies are some of the most fun lore I've had the pleasure of sifting though. 

Gameplay wise, it's fine. The guns feel weak and inaccurate, but the the powers are good fun. Launch is essentially the gravity gun and your best offensive tool, whereby you'll fling everything from filing cabins to corpses at a decent number of unique, though someone uninspired, enemies. 

While  there's a variety of different enemies to encounter, your tactics will hardly need refining as they barely elicit different responses to be defeated.
Strange mind twisting story telling but gameplay is fun as hell.  Will finish it but not sure if I would ever come back to it. Recommand it!

It's a good game. It's very rich with content and optional missions.
There's also some elements of horror, but it's definitely not scary.
The one thing that I hate about it the most is the map structure.
I'm not really a fan of this game, but it's pretty good.
Action packed and an absurdly weird story that absolutely works. The best of both worlds indeed. Great game altogether.
老游戏便宜又好玩。美术和剧情风格独特。我喜欢在低功耗机器上玩游戏，而这游戏在基础款m4 mac mini上运行的非常好。但因为不支持FSR，在AMD处理器的掌机上表现很差。

In general, Control Ultimate Edition's one of the most unique & immersive action games I've played. The combat feels incredibly satisfying, especially with the telekinetic powers that let ya throw objects around the environment. The story is mysterious & keeps you wanting to uncover more secrets while the atmosphere of the Oldest House is both creepy and fascinating. The Ultimate Edition also includes all DLCs, adding even more content and lore to explore. Great visuals, excellent gameplay, and a memorable world make this a must-play for fans of action & supernatural sci-fi games. 9/10 for me! 👍🔥

I don't even know where to start. The story is genuinely one of the most ambitious, weird, and brilliantly structured narratives I've experienced in a game. Everything connects. Every side mission feeds back into the main story, nothing feels like filler, nothing feels disconnected. It's linear, but in the best possible way. You're always moving forward and it always means something.
The combat is where this game truly goes insane. Telekinesis, levitation, the ability to rip objects out of the environment mid-fight, it feels absolutely unhinged in the best way. And the visual effects when bullets land? The particle debris, the dust, the environmental destruction reacting in real time? It's one of the most satisfying feedback loops I've felt in a third-person shooter. Every fight feels cinematic without trying to be.
Cutscenes are minimal, but that's the point. When they hit, they genuinely hit. No bloat, no skippable filler, just carefully placed moments that land every single time.
For a game this cheap, the level of craft here is almost unreasonable. Nothing feels rushed, nothing feels unfinished. Zero bugs in my entire 18-hour playthrough. Remedy clearly gave everything they had.
Slept on masterpiece. No notes.
Great atmospheric story with fun gameplay mechanics that goes beyond just point and shoot

Love the ambience, love the storytelling, it definitely wont be for everyone but if you love psychological horror with some shooter gameplay, this is the one.

note: the expansion had some connection with the Alan Wake, so you better play that game first before play the expansion

Good, game, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ love Remedy's world building and art direction,  i just wish they did something more unique with the gameplay instead of well executed but fairly generic feeling superhero action combat. Also they know how to write well, and the writing is often excellent, but they also have a tendency to overexplain and be extremely redundant specifically w the main plot and Jesse's internal monologue, which is unfortunate when the game is trying to be in a genre who's best entries are marked by their tendency to not explain things, which is something they are good at doing in the side content. Also, Girl, you can shut the ♥♥♥♥ up when i take more than 3 seconds on a puzzle. And you dont need to be like "should i tell her?" 6 times in every dialog scene.

Control - 9/10

Control is cool as hell. I don't think I've ever played a game more "cinematic" that actually had a lot of game to it. There were so many moments throughout that just made me go "woah", and it doesn't waste any time getting to the good stuff.  The story is serviceable, nothing I haven't seen before, but it's world is super cool and it tackles some interesting themes. The music is fantastic and the sound design stands out as being superb. The only real complaint I have is over some of the enemy and encounter design. The checkpoints mean you have to do the walk of shame back to an encounter after you lose and the enemy throws are difficult to dodge and will take you down to one HP. Otherwise Control is short, sweet, and has style like I've never seen in a game.

so far I'm enjoying it as a decent cinematic experience. 
combats fun, upgrade system is cool, cosmetics i could care less but they exist...
a nice and shiny story game if you like ray tracing and trippy visual design.
kind've got RE vibes i say that because you have to look for keycards to get to different areas and then go back to earlier areas and use them etc.
Great game, amazing visuals, excited for the next one!!

Someone recommended this to me and I'm happy about it. I think it's an awesome game. Still looks good, plays great, nice action/story/puzzle mix that keeps me hooked for quite a few hours. Excited for what will come next soon!

This game is crazy even my brother liked it and he doesn't play singleplayer 
respect remedy entertainment
Absolute banger of a game, holds up incredibly well

GET READING, DIRECTOR!!!!

In all seriousness, Control has a mostly linear story that's easy to follow even if you've never played an Alan Wake game. The world-building is fantastic, the combat feels satisfying, and the overall presentation is top-notch.

My favorite part was definitely the lore scattered throughout the world. The documents, recordings, and case files make exploration incredibly rewarding. Let's just say the Federal Bureau of Control has an absolutely ridiculous amount of juicy workplace tea, and I was here for all of it.

My one complaint is that the combat and gameplay can start to feel a bit repetitive toward the end. That said, if you're not the type of player who feels compelled to 100% every area and collect every last item, it's unlikely to bother you much.

If you're a fan of the Alan Wake universe, enjoy digging through lore, or just like exploring maps packed with secrets and side content, you really can't go wrong with this one.

Game is fun but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ does the UX suck ass.  You will run around in circles trying to figure out how to reach a quest marker before resorting to a youtube video to figure out what should have just been communicated through the map. Still, would recommend.

A very good game that has a couple small issues. Mainly some strange leveling on enemies and a overreliance on throwing items at them. Had a couple visual bugs as well. Still the game was worth playing throughout these minor issues. Very interesting story and the gameplay is very fun. Dlcs are worth getting as well though the story in Foundation isn't exactly its strong suit. Visually one of the best looking games of 2019 and one of the best games from Remedy yet. Sequel is a day one buy for me

Great atmosphere and art design, as well as great combat mechanics. Not a lot of games let you straight fly around the levels and the Half-Life 2-esque physics gameplay is awesome. The action gameplay is slightly let down with weak enemy AI and a lack of enemy variety. I thought the story was pretty good and excellent for a game and the world building is consistently compelling. The voice actors are obviously very good, but sometimes the direction is strange with too much of a disinterested vibe and lack of intonation. The cutscenes, especially the live action ones, are significantly better though and often times hilarious. The only thing that really brings the game down is the uninteresting boss design. They're spongy and often come with gimmick mechanics. Luckily they are a small part of the game and almost entirely related to side quests and therefore optional. Some people thought the ending was weak, but I personally liked it a lot. And now that we're getting a sequel the complaints will be less pertinent. 

8/10
Interesting lore and world building. Great gunplay overall despite ocasional spikes that feel unfair.
This game is so cool. I love the atmosphere and story, Jesse is a great character, and the gameplay is so fun. I highly recommend it!

I LOVE REMEDY! This game is magnificent. Not ideal, especially the ending of the main campaign and the two DLCs "Foundation" and "AWE", but for me it's a very very good game. In the first hours of the game, I was just breaking everything around, like in childhood when discovering a new game and felt the same emotions. I liked the atmosphere and read/listened to everything I found. The gameplay was also interesting, especially when you discover the levitating power, but maybe they could have made the game harder. I definitely recommend this game, not because it's paired with Alan Wake, but because it's good on its own
this game is soo good first it kinda scare but remedy made this game soo cool and when it comes to end remedy made the game contiuation in the credits also thats soo impressive nice game

Looks great, runs great, combat is super satisfying.
Environment is destructible and usable against your enemies.
Excited for the sequel.
The vibe of Twin Peaks and the X-Files with genuinely fun gameplay. Narratively a good entry point into the Remedyverse since it's more modern and stands on its own well.

its a must play, it its on sale do yourself a favor and pick it up. Alan Wake 1/2 not my type of games but the story is good so seeing that this was a power fantasy shooter in the same universe i had to give it a try and was not disappointed.

Pros:
- Fun Fighting Mechanics
- Well Paced Progression (Tons of Exploration)
- Story Rich (Good DLC's)
- New Unique Form of Horror/Sci-Fi to Be Explored
- Amazing Graphics
Cons: 
- Tedious Map Traversing (Certain Parts)
- Inconsistent Auto Saves

After finally giving this game a chance I am happy to say that it was overall great experience. Control does a great job at utilizing aspects from other Sci-Fi games and adding its own unique twist to it. The weapon mechanics were  very enjoyable and satisfying to customize/adapt whenever against a new foe. The games story was weird... but in a good way! I technically finished the games main story after about 14 hours but due to how interesting the game is I decided to pursue all the side missions and even do the DLC's that came with the Ultimate Edition of the game.  The DLC's were a blast and offered different genres and new mechanics with them too. The map exploration was both a blessing and a curse, big and full of stuff to find while also becoming a maze where the map didn't really help much. Once you learn the maps from doing missions that reoccur in certain areas I began to not worry about it as much. Sometimes whenever I would die and respawn from my last saved location it would be either just before the fight or I would end up having to go through a decent chunk of the map to return where I last was at due to those inconsistent saves. 

Overall, I give this game a solid 8.5!
Tons of content with great atmosphere.  The combat was enjoyable to me with lots of customization available for your play style.

the game is amazing from the first few hours i was hooked but damn around an hour it starts to stutter badly. Tried anything to fix this cause the game runs fine around 90 fps but it suddenly drops at 9 fps choppy gameplay but still a good game i had to refund it but i hope they fix the issue in the future

The greatest thing about this game in my opinion, apart from the general polish, is how despite primarily being a shooting game its so much more than just the typical one headshot and it's done kind of thing. The guns and different character abilities always manages to keep things fresh and different in a challenging manner.
Great ambiance, great gameplay, good story. However it can be a bit difficult to catch whats going on when everything is blowing up

Overall, it's a pretty good game.

It's got an, honestly, really cool story. The lore is extremely interesting, it's mysterious but not too mysterious, there's intrigue and confusion and twists and everything. Really well done with the story, lore, and writing.

But dude. There is so much f***ing backtracking it is unreal. I despise inefficient and unnecessary backtracking. It is just cringe and meant to artificially increase playtime. Sometimes you find something you don't have the power for or you find a door that you can't unlock yet because you don't have the keycard for it and you have to come back way later. Even worse when you realize the map does not show locked doors, so you have to watch a youtube video, remember exactly where it is, or just backtrack through the entire area to find the doors. Extremely annoying.

The gameplay is also pretty good, and it's honestly fun, but a little bit shallow. The game gives you a bunch of cool little powers, but I really never had to use more than the very first one they give, because the game is extremely easy. I mean the only time I ever died was dashing off the platform because I'm an idiot. The gameplay is stupidly easy, even the challenge mode extra stuff is easy. Also the gun and the lore with that is pretty sick, and the forms of the gun are cool, but again it's just kinda lacking in terms of upgrades. This games version of experience points become effectively worthless after a certain point in the game.

The sound design, just like Alan Wake, is fantastic. Literally can't say anything bad about the sound design. The voice acting is also actually pretty good. Except in one of the DLCs because the Alan Wake actor is back and I guess in the 9 years between the DLC and Alan Wake 1, he never figured out how to stop talking like he is simultaneously constipated and doing a Solid Snake impression.

The DLCs are pretty mediocre, unfortunately. The first one, which comes after the game has finished, is pretty cool but has honestly one of the worst endings I've seen in recent memory. You basically fix the issue, and then you are IMMEDIATELY (I mean like you walk maybe 30 seconds) presented with a new threat who at that point you kinda know what his deal is and that he isn't strictly a good guy. And then. After that. The game just plays the final cutscene. Like brother? Hello? There is a Statue of Liberty sized octopus demon in the abyss and we are just ignoring that? The Alan Wake focused DLC is also not great. It's really cool if you've played Alan Wake 1 and know the story already, but if you don't then you literally have no idea what is happening or why you should care. It feels shoe-horned into the game to promote Alan Wake 2 which was in production at the time.

Main game ending is pretty sick though.

Final thing is that because this game is from 2019, DLSS doesn't work right, which is a little annoying. Game is also stutters quite a bit when it has been open for a long time, though this might be because I played on borderless fullscreen. Regardless, a bit annoying.

7.5/10 - worth the $40 it's listed at.
The reason I then got alan wake and alan wake 2 and the rest of the remedy games. Going to play a 2nd time before the next game comes out

Control is awesome! The story is fantastic, the animation is incredible, and the gameplay is fun and rewarding, but challenging at the same time. The best thing for me is you can tailor the weapons, the powers and the upgrades to your playstyle. And you can mix and match with different combinations, that you can change during combat. Thanks Remedy for creating such a gem!
Very good, even if the ending to the story felt a bit abrupt and incomplete. Looking forward to the sequel.

Crazyy game 10/10..atmosphere , environment , cinematics , storytelling , everything was beyond amazing. Gotta admit this game right here is the "Inception" of games , obviously I coudn't unsderstand the story at all..the lore is really complex , now I will watch an explanation video to truly the game...but overall it was such an amazing game , some fight sections are also brutal as hell..anyways..Remedy kudos to you ;)

Unbelievable storytelling from Remedy as always, a phenomenal action packed thriller with an outstanding world design and charmingly memorable characters. Secretly a horror game in disguise because of how intense the atmosphere and tension gets when exploring. Can't wait to see how the Alan Wake games continue to connect to this shared universe.

A decent game but the closer I got to finishing it the more I just wanted it to be over so I could uninstall it. The map design is awful and the characters and dialogue are just nonsensical at times and its more apparent as the game progresses. It was also tedious having to fight or evade the many mobs that respawn every time when you traverse between areas, which you have to do quite a few times. The gameplay was still the most enjoyable part. Wait for a discount on this one.
pretty solid gameplay loop, finally playable framerates are possible with the game still looking good
Game is great, story is very interesting however the fights are bit repetetive.

All about the effects. The feel of the gun, the atmosphere and especially the feel of the telekinetic powers.
Definitely close to the top for me in that regard.

A nice and surreal action experience.
Like any Remedy game, there's a lot of care and thought put into any miniscule detail, whether it's in environments, logs or papers found throughout the map, or the side-quests with their own storylines.
The story is bizarre but engaging, the music accentuates the sense of horror/dread inside the Old House and the controls feel excellent.
One criticism I often heard before playing this game is that it can feel repetitive, but I gotta say, I don't share this sentiment. The game works as an action RPG where you run around the offices and encounter enemies in combat arenas. The good thing is that there are lots of enemy types, introduced at a right pace, and you unlock new weapons and abilities that can be upgradeable. So, at least for me, the game does bring something new to keep you engaged through it's runtime.
If there is one thing I wished the game had, is the ability to replay missions, as there are some standouts I'd wish to replay without having to restart the whole game to play them.
Another masterpiece from Remedy

Great aesthetic
Great gameplay
Okayish story but love the vibe and how they  have implemented the "office" feeling into paranormal activities.
Amazing atmosphere and solid gameplay. Great before a trip to the sauna.
great game, only problem is that some puzzles are unsolvable because my gpu isnt pwerful enough to render the whiteboards with hints, which is kinda bull
The art style is crepy and ominous which suits the game but takes a bit to grow on you. The story is excellent and it does great justice to the psyhological horror genre.

Control has become of of my favorite games of all time.  Why?  The Presentation.

The story is serviceable, and the combat is good.  But the style is incredible.  I love The Oldest House.  I love the brutalist architechture.  I love the office spaces furnished with anachronistic equipment.  I love wandering the reality-defying layout and finding tucked-away nooks and hidden offices ( I want that office above Dead Letters!).

I love the SCP-style mysteries and sifting through the memos of the agents trying to cope with confronting things beyond mortal ken.  It's not a perfect game by any means, but there's nothing else quite like it.

A very good game with a very rich and interesting story, the people who made this game are very creative and have an absurd imagination, I can't believe I managed to get this magnificent work of art for such a low price. I can't wait to play the sequel and see how Jesse and Dylan's story ends.

firstly, i would like to appreciate the devs of this game to make it playable on Mac OS, i know that sounds weird but i have my reasons, 

anyways, the game is great, got hooked on after around 2.5 hrs of play, it could be a little scary in some moments, idk if it was meant to be or am just a coward LOL, really enjoyed playing this game, uh the last hiss enemies were the worst those with the pickaxe, dude screw them.

One of my top 10 favorite games to have played. The only small downside to it is that I wish that all of the steam achievements were working when I had first played  the game. If they had been I would have 100% the game. 

Worth the price, if you've never played it before!

What I liked:
‣ Great art direction.
‣ Great graphics, good lighting, good sound design, and really smooth transitions.
‣ Decent story with a really, really good atmosphere.
‣ Fun and interactive gameplay (satisfying combat and engaging puzzles).
‣ Has meaningful character and weapon upgrades.
‣ Has endgame content.

Mixed:
‣ Hard to navigate the map early on (you get used to it once you learn to read the signs on the walls).
‣ Difficulty spikes (sometimes I started missions that were overleveled for my character).

Overall, it’s quite a good game that gets you immersed and invested in either its gameplay or story, with lots of things to discover across a few compact maps. It reminds me of Quantum Break but with more freedom, so if you liked that, you’ll probably like this one too.
Am on my second playthrough nearly two years after playing it through the first time.  One of the best physics based games i have played.
Great story, neat environment design, surprisingly pretty solid shooter. Enjoyed this game a lot.
Amazing graphics and great combat, even if does get very repetitive (to be expected from remedy)

Control is not a bad game at all. The atmosphere, the weirdness, and the bits of dry humor kept me interested early on. Around the halfway point, the combat starts to feel repetitive, and I reached the point where I thought, “Just let me finish the game already.”

The story is strong, filled with strange lore and a surreal tone that Remedy handles well. If you can push through the increasingly tedious fights, the overall experience is still worth a playthrough.

a masterclass in videogame ambiance. The characteristic sandbox-like environment of remedy games is definitely present and the gameplay is incredibly satisfying. The graphics definitely hold up even after 7 years, maybe even better than most recent games, especially in matters of lighting. The best part is definitely the story though. (buy it, stop hesitating)
The most mind-bending and polished game I've ever played. The art direction is superb, the acting and the music is top notch. Can't wait to play other Remedy games.

Imagine waking up from the strangest, most disturbing, and abstract dream—one you actually had control of and remember perfectly. 

The dream was bizarre, defying all logic, yet it made complete sense to you. 

Now, imagine trying to explain it to someone else without sounding like a total lunatic.

​This is Control. 

If you think you know what 'weird' and 'disturbing' is, then sit the fk down as this game will redefine them for you. A schizophrenic stream of consciousness blended with pure entropy...

...and some shooting.

10/10

jesse faden is so cool
also the game is good and looks awesome or something
if you experience stutters while playing, make sure to switch to dx11. u won't get hdr or ray tracing but its worth it, believe me. the stutters suck

Threshold Kids FTW !

9/10

Edit: I would give it a 10/10 if they would not put a game ad in this game for Alan Wake 2 which is not even available on Stream. Stop it.

love this game.
graphics still great in 2026.
gameplay and storyline is awesome.
remind me abit of Stranger Things

If you are a fan of 3rd person shooter this game is a must play. Absolutely awsome game. The story can't get any wierder but thats the charm of the game. Graphics, sound design, level design, characters, power sets and everything about this game is as weird as it can get and as awsome as it can be.
This game makes me feel like a Jedi. Really I can levitate, Force push and make object float, shoot some weird gun. Damn I wish we get a Star wars game from Remedy. It will be cool.

PS: I already finished the game once while adding this review. But the first time I played a free copy of Control I got from Epic. But I loved the game so much I decided to buy it in steam to support the developer and to write a review. Cheers.....

It's the first time I play a game like this, and it just pulls me in so immediately. I'd never heard of the game, never played anything even similar (I'm more of an e-sport gamer). It was on a big discount, so I bought it and didn't think much of it, I wanted to at least run it once, plus it would be the newest game I run with my 4070ti SOCE.

And I just... Kept playing.

And when I put it down, I just kept coming back for more.
It took me years to finish Witcher 3 and Death Stranding (the only other non esports adjacent games I've really played), and it was mostly because I would have to either wait for a Holliday or just a day I had enough free time to spare, this game doesn't care. I'ts so easy to just pick it up and play for a while. I can't tell you why.

Wanted to play before sequel come out. What a masterpiece. The story and lore behind it is very intriguing. The world building is incredible. It becomes too much reading time to time, especially to a modern audience. But each note adds tiny bit of realism to the world with such intrigue that I was often wondering if said note could become a spin off on its own.
They really thought some things through.
I love when a game or story makes you wish had wrote it.

I don't really have the energy to type a full review right now but I wanted to leave a thumbs up and say just how thoroughly I enjoyed this game. I rarely put more than 2 or 3 hours into any one game. I'm at 40 and going with this one. Can't wait for the sequel. 

Pick this game up at full price, or when it goes on sale. Either way - I believe it to be an enjoyable and mysterious experience that many could appreciate.
A good 3PS with varied gameplay and a great SCP-inspired story. The difficulty sliders make it accessible for pretty much everyone.
My personal 2019 GOTY and 2nd favorite game. i've got 2 games on the number 1 spot.

Control continues Remedy's method of crytic storytelling with confusing visuals and gameplay.

To me Control is probably one of the weaker entries due to the repition of the gameplay. It is fun to play through but there is not enough variation to me to justify just how much of it there is, by the end of the game I did not want to fight enemies and avoided fights as much as I could.

The story to me is the weakest and the strongest points of the game, that take you throughout a creativly built world. But the main villain was pretty lackluster and I found the side villain for the Alan Wake dlc to be far more compelling.

I do recommend playing through Control, but I think taking it slow over a long period of time is probably the best method of playing it.
Amazing concept, aesthetic, and combat system.

It’s an entire vibe. It throws you into a brutalist, reality-bending, bureaucratic fever dream as Jesse, It easily stands out as one of the most inventive action-adventure titles and I just finally beat it 6 years later.

a high-stress corporate simulator where HR is a floating red entity, the office building actively rewrites its own layout, and your primary job qualification is the ability to hurl office furniture at your coworkers using your mind.

I love this game, what a spectacle 

It aged really well, and its vibe and presentation are sublime. It is by far my favorite Remedy title

Grab the ultimate edition during a sale and enjoy unprecedented [REDACTED]
Excelente juego de acción, ciencia ficción y aventura. La historia es relativamente simple pero la plantean como si fuera muy compleja.Buenos gráficos, buenas mecánicas. Es un juego divertido.
genuinely one of my favorite games, and another certified Remedy classic. There's a reason people were so hype about this game when it came out...

GENUINELY INCREDIBLE GAME!!! Stunning visuals, excellent sound design, and charming characters come together to form a narrative that somehow keeps you in the loop of the story while making sure you have NO idea what's going on. In the late game there are a couple of side quests and random enemy encounters that can get a little repetitive, but in truth, it ends up just being an excuse to explore the incredibly detailed world within The Oldest House. Not to mention, the majority of side quests have a variety of places to explore, mechanics to utilize, and unique storylines and B plots. Without any spoilers - my favorite character is Ahti, my favorite place is the Ashtray Maze, my favorite Boss is the Former, and my favorite side quest was the one with the movie camera. Any of that sound interesting? I implore you, PLEASE play this game and explore every corner you can. 9.5/10 worms through time, would ABSOLUTELY play through again. Might 100% it later.

Great game, gameplay is top notch and has better powers than most superhero games. One of the coolest settings I've seen in a game too with very intriguing lore. Remedy make some of the most unique games in the industry. Can't wait for the sequel.
